rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 22, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER GOLDEN PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE JUDGMENT is OFF CALENDAR. Notice of Appeal on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANTS ANNA PAPOULIAS, VICTOR G. MAKRAS AND VICTOR G. MAKRAS, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PREVAILING PARTY ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS

rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. KEVIN J. HESLIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Quiet Title
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 22, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CHOPKO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO QUIET TITLE is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist whether the operative Note and the Deed of Trust are void (Lona v. Citibank, N.A. (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 89) defeating the title of a bona fide purchaser. Firato v. rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. EARL MORGAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice And Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 22, 2022 line 8. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE AND MOTION FOR ENFORCEMENT OF SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is OFF CALENDAR, insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1010.6. The Court further notes that the Proof of Service on file does not comply with all requirements for e-service proof of service (e.g. address of the sender is not listed). The Court notes that if Plaintiff intends to re-file this motion P must address the procedural posture of this case and cite authority addressing the enforceability of the settlement agreement resolving the original complaint in light of the filing of the new complaint and new answer, and judge Ulmer's order re: abandonment of the issues raised by the original pleading and requirement for a new round of pleadings; as well as the finding that the other issues raised (enforcement of the settlement) are moot in light of the filing of an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. VICTOR ANDINO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 22, 2022 line 11. DEFENDANT VICTOR ANDINO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice states multiple grounds and provides factual assertions as to several violations. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that the notice is invalid if one of the assertions is not supported or that the inclusion of such assertion renders the notice uncertain within the meaning of CCP 430.10(f). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
